note: Explorer UI fixes (v11-phase2). Two issues reported on the live explorer: (1) HIDE NAV button only hid ConvergenceX + PoPC DEX logos but left the SOST logo, the SOST EXPLORER label, the MAINNET badge, and the entire right-side header-link group (CONVERGENCEX, GEASPIRIT, MATERIALS ENGINE, AI ENGINE, WEBSITE, APP, GITHUB) still visible. Cause: js/nav-toggle.js' collapse CSS only targeted .nav-logo / .nav-links / .nav-hamburger / a[href=casert-spec.html] / a[href=sost-dex.html] — the explorer page uses inline styles instead of those classes, so most of its nav was untouched by the rule. Fix: added 'body.nav-collapsed nav > div:not(.container) { display: none !important }' to nav-toggle.js. The :not(.container) qualifier targets the explorer's structure (<nav><div style=...>) without affecting the homepage or the other 45 section pages (which use <nav><div class=container>). The toggle button is appended directly to <nav> as a sibling of the inner div on the explorer, so it stays visible. (2) NES chiptune button: defensive rewrite of the inline click handler — explicit error logging via console.error instead of swallowed exceptions, fallback chain (sost-music.js -> retro-sounds.js -> visual error indicator on the button), single-click plays a fresh melody every time. Hard reload (Ctrl+Shift+R).
